title: EVPN/VXLAN Tunnel Health
spl:
index=network (sourcetype="evpn:bgp" OR sourcetype="vxlan:tunnel") earliest=-24h
| eval bad=if(match(lower(state),"(?i)down|idle") OR error_count>0,1,0)
| where bad=1
| stats latest(state) as st, sum(error_count) as err by vni, peer_ip, leaf
| table leaf, peer_ip, vni, st, err
description: Unified view of BGP EVPN tunnel state and VXLAN encapsulation errors per VNI — bridges overlay protocols (complements UC-18.3.4 and UC-18.3.9).
value: Unified view of BGP EVPN tunnel state and VXLAN encapsulation errors per VNI — bridges overlay protocols (complements UC-18.3.4 and UC-18.3.9).
implementation: Normalize peer and VNI from vendor logs. Alert on any down EVPN session carrying VXLAN for production VNIs.
visualization: Table (unhealthy tunnels), Geo/leaf map, Line chart (error count).
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: `TA-cisco_ios` (NX-OS), `arista:eos` via SC4S, BGP telemetry.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `evpn:bgp`, `vxlan:tunnel`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Normalize peer and VNI from vendor logs. Alert on any down EVPN session carrying VXLAN for production VNIs.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=network (sourcetype="evpn:bgp" OR sourcetype="vxlan:tunnel") earliest=-24h
| eval bad=if(match(lower(state),"(?i)down|idle") OR error_count>0,1,0)
| where bad=1
| stats latest(state) as st, sum(error_count) as err by vni, peer_ip, leaf
| table leaf, peer_ip, vni, st, err
```

Understanding this SPL

**EVPN/VXLAN Tunnel Health** — Unified view of BGP EVPN tunnel state and VXLAN encapsulation errors per VNI — bridges overlay protocols (complements UC-18.3.4 and UC-18.3.9).

Documented **Data sources**: `evpn:bgp`, `vxlan:tunnel`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `TA-cisco_ios` (NX-OS), `arista:eos` via SC4S, BGP telemetry. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: network; **sourcetype**: evpn:bgp, vxlan:tunnel. Those sourcetypes align with what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=network, sourcetype="evpn:bgp", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **bad** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where bad=1` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by vni, peer_ip, leaf** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Pipeline stage (see **EVPN/VXLAN Tunnel Health**): table leaf, peer_ip, vni, st, err


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm results in the index, then compare with the same scope in the relevant fabric/SDN controller or manager UI.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (unhealthy tunnels), Geo/leaf map, Line chart (error count).
grandmaExplanation: Unified view of EVPN tunnel state and VXLAN encapsulation errors per VNI — bridges overlay protocols (complements UC-18.3.4 and UC-18.3.9) — so traffic problems, routing mistakes, and risky changes surface fast.
knownFalsePositives: Faults during planned maintenance, hardware refresh, or after firmware upgrades.
